projects.title: 3DOF Reaction Control System
projects.subtitle: Cold Gas Nitrogen Thruster Rig
projects.description: Sole design engineer for a new-generation 3DOF RCS test rig using cold gas nitrogen thrusters. Developed mechanical constraint equations to determine required thrust, operating pressure, and mass flow with hand calculations and MATLAB simulation. Modified TVC gimbal for pitch and yaw with 60° operating cone. Determined a 2-passage rotary union within budget and physical constraints, converting it into a controller for 8 separate fluid lines. Designed an innovative slotted rack electronics board prioritizing serviceability and ease of wiring. System validated as an inverted pendulum for attitude and roll stabilization. Conducted valve testing and thruster impulse characterization for thrust commands at 600 psi.

projects.title: Front Unsprung Assembly
projects.subtitle: 2024–25 FSAE Electric Car
projects.description: Designed, fabricated, and assembled the front unsprung assemblies for the 2024–25 UT FSAE Electric car. Milled 7075 aluminum construction for lightweight unsprung mass. Designed for modularity and adjustability for camber adjustment and Ackermann steering geometry. Hub assembly with precision-selected bearings with SKF bearing simulations, and custom hub nut for optimal stiffness and performance. Developed custom bearing seals and unsprung enclosures to protect critical components and house sensors. Implemented fatigue analysis with a minimum factor of safety of 2 under various load conditions. Redesigned hub architecture and upright mounts, achieving 33% weight reduction while maintaining engineering stiffness requirements. Developed an original magnetic flux analytical framework to redesign wheel speed sensor architecture with 3-phase hall effect sensor array, achieving 2.5–5x signal amplitude improvement over prior design.

specs.val: Precision-selected Angular Contact BB, custom seals

projects.title: Rear Upright Assembly
projects.subtitle: 2023–24 FSAE Electric Car
projects.description: Designed, fabricated, and assembled the rear unsprung assemblies for the 2023–24 UT FSAE Electric car. Milled 7075 aluminum for lightweight construction with great stiffness-to-weight ratio. Specialized tripod interface with steel inserts to efficiently transmit motor torque through half shafts. Developed adjustable camber and toe systems for precise suspension tuning. Comprehensive FEA including advanced modeling of bearing interactions and load transfers of suspension linkages. Developed solutions for integrating wheel speed measurement sensors within the rear unsprung assembly. Redesigned upright architecture and brake mounting, achieving a 44% decrease in rear unsprung subsystem mass.

projects.title: 2023 Robot
projects.subtitle: FRC World Championship — Galileo Division
projects.description: 2023 Excellence in Engineering Award, FRC World Championship, Galileo Division; Award presented to 8 teams globally, recognizing technical prowess of the robot and the team's ability to document and articulate their engineering decisions. Full in-house manufacturing using box and plate aluminum 6061 and carbon-nylon composite 3D prints. Integrated magnet encoder sensors throughout for precision control and system feedback. Double-jointed arm with vertical carriage allowing full pass-through capabilities and scoring at any location. Compact 23" × 23" drivetrain with steel ballasts for high maneuverability. 8-motor swerve drive system capable of high-speed omnidirectional movement.

projects.title: 2023 Arm + Elevator
projects.subtitle: Single-Stage Carriage Elevator & Double-Jointed Arm
projects.description: Designed and fabricated a robust single-stage carriage elevator with double-jointed arm subsystem. 47" 3"×3" truss box tube with SOLIDWORKS FEA stiffness analysis across multiple load cases. 17 lb Constant force spring for net-zero carriage weight with 0.32s carriage travel time. 6061 aluminum tie rod truss structure for rigidity during high-acceleration swing movements. Compact 75:1 carriage gearbox with cam & chain tensioner for shoulder rotation (±180°). Custom carbon-nylon herringbone gear profile for wrist control (±270°). 37" dual carbon fiber rod arm with bond-area calculated aluminum and carbon-nylon end supports.

projects.title: 2022 Robot + Shooter
projects.subtitle: Turret System with Integrated Vision
projects.description: 2022 UIL State Champion (1 of 1 in Texas) · Worlds Roebling Division Semifinalist · 2× Excellence in Engineering Award · 3× 1st Seed · Autonomous Award. Undefeated through Dripping Springs District qualifications and playoffs; 1st seed and event winner at Austin District; undefeated in UIL State qualifications. Designed a versatile, fast-moving competition robot with precision shooting system. 6061 aluminum box and plate with polycarbonate construction. Highly modular design with independent subsystems for fast serviceability. Dual flywheel system with reduced backspin and precision turret achieving 180° motion and 95% shot accuracy via integrated vision controlling flywheel RPM, turret angle, and 24° hood travel through a high-reduction carbon-nylon sector gear gearbox.

specs.val: UIL State Champion · 2× Excellence in Engineering · Autonomous Award

projects.title: 2021 Indexer Mechanism
projects.subtitle: Revolving 6-Element Storage System
projects.description: Designed and fabricated a revolving indexing system storing 6 dodgeball game elements for fast-rate scoring. CNC-routed lightweight polycarbonate structure with carbon-nylon 3D print components. Compact internalized gearbox with less than 2" total height for in-chassis placement. Bearing stackup constrains translation and allows Z-axis rotation under large axial and radial loads. Polycarbonate drum controls position and flow rate of stored game elements. 1-screw quick-release drum for full robot serviceability.

projects.title: 2021 High Rate Indexer
projects.subtitle: Dual-Motor Feeder Subsystem
projects.description: Designed a high-rate indexing subsystem for increased control and speed in a lightweight, robust package. Dual motor feeder mechanism allows individual control and fast intake of stored game elements. Lightweight partial-pocketed drum with .040" polycarbonate wall supported by 7075 aluminum standoffs. Fully encased design with dead axle structural center rod supporting drum rotation and feeder mechanism with carbon-nylon end supports. Single stage, high torque drum gearbox with bearing stackup for large radial and axial loads.

projects.title: RC Race Car
projects.subtitle: Custom Timing Belt Drivetrain & Rack & Pinion Steering
projects.description: End-to-end design, manufacture, and assembly of a high-performance RC race car. Custom axles manually machined on lathe with M4 tapped ends. Laser-cut plywood base with snap-fit wood joints, cantilever-style upright mounts, and low-pressure tires for suspension-free shock absorption. Custom 3D-printed servo-powered rack & pinion steering. RWD belt drivetrain with 3:1 gear ratio, solid rear axle, and custom 3D-printed pulleys. Ackermann steering optimized for 60° inner and 37° outer angles, achieving 0.41m turning radius. Achieved 5 m/s top speed and 19.83s qualifying time for 3rd place finish.

projects.title: Smokeless Fire Pit
projects.subtitle: High-Rate Production Sheet Metal Design
projects.description: Designed a durable, stainless steel smokeless fire pit minimizing smoke output for a cleaner outdoor experience. Full 316L stainless steel sheet metal construction in .048", 0.090", and 0.12" sheets. Integrated tolerances for bending, die shaping, and rolling processes ensuring structural integrity in high-heat environments and ease of assembly for high-rate production. Fully parametric SOLIDWORKS model for adjustable diameter, height, and airflow. Created initial prototype, iterating design based on real-world thermal performance data.

projects.title: Face Shield Production
projects.subtitle: High-Rate FDM Production for First Responders
projects.description: Designed and fabricated two face shield variants for first responder distribution during COVID-19. Standard model uses polycarbonate shield with elastic headband for ease of assembly. Folding model achieves 88% reduction in unit print time vs. standard model, enabling scaled FDM production at high throughput. Polycarbonate shields die-cut for consistent, high-quantity output.
